title: Luscious Creamy Syrian Kombu Stew
description: A savory, creamy, lazy-sunday stew that blends fermented kombu with chickpeas and coconut milk for a comforting Syrian-inspired dish.
instructions: Rinse the dried kombu in cool water, then soak in 2 cups of warm water for 20 minutes to rehydrate.
Drain and finely chop the kombu into a small dice.
Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the chopped kombu, chickpeas, tomato paste, ground cumin, coriander, and za'atar; stir to coat and cook for 2 minutes.
Pour in 200 ml of coconut milk and 50 ml of water, bring to a gentle simmer, and let it thicken for 10 minutes.
Whisk in 2 tablespoons of tahini to create a creamy texture, then season with salt and pepper to taste.
Finish with a squeeze of fresh lemon juice, stir gently, and remove from heat.
Serve hot with a drizzle of olive oil and a sprinkle of fresh parsley if desired.
